# ASAP Pest & Wildlife Removal — Complete Site Specification (Machine-Readable)

> Humane wildlife removal and pest control in Metro Atlanta — inspect, trap, exclude, disinfect. Licensed by Georgia DNR and Georgia Department of Agriculture. Available 24 hours a day, 6 days a week.

## Business

- Legal Name: ASAP Pest & Wildlife Removal
- Friendly Name: ASAP Pest & Wildlife
- Industry: Wildlife Removal and Pest Control
- Business Type: Family-run, owner-operated
- Headquarters: Atlanta, Georgia, USA
- Service Region: Metro Atlanta and surrounding Georgia counties
- Service Areas: Cobb, Fulton, Forsyth, Cherokee, DeKalb, Gwinnett, Fayette, and adjacent counties
- Address: 2990 Pine Canyon Dr SW, Unit 11, Atlanta, GA 30331
- Phone: (770) 450-1744
- Email: info@removeasap.com
- Website: https://removeasap.com
- Hours: 24 hours a day, 6 days a week (emergency response)

## Core Offer: The Peace-of-Mind Wildlife Shield

### Promise
One call. Problem solved. Peace restored.

### Mechanism — The Four-Step Complete Solution
Most wildlife companies only do steps 1 and 2. We do all four. This is why our work lasts.

1. **Inspect** — Licensed technician identifies the animal, locates entry points, documents damage, and assesses health risks. Inspection fee is credited toward service if you proceed.
2. **Trap** — Humane trapping based on the animal's movement patterns. Traps are monitored regularly until the problem is fully resolved. No poisons. No cruelty.
3. **Exclude** — Every entry point and weak spot is sealed so animals cannot get back in. This is the step most companies skip — and the reason problems return.
4. **Disinfect** — Attic, crawl space, vents, and affected areas are cleaned and disinfected to remove health hazards from droppings, urine, and nesting materials.

### Service Catalog

| Service | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| Wildlife Removal | Raccoons, squirrels, opossums, bats, rats, mice, snakes, armadillos, coyotes, beavers, birds, bees, wasps, hornets, and more. Humane methods, licensed by Georgia DNR. |
| Residential Pest Control | Ongoing treatment and prevention for homes. Eco-friendly methods. Customized plans. |
| Commercial Pest Control | Scheduled, discreet service for businesses, property managers, apartment communities, airports, hotels, schools, and hospitals. Documentation and compliance reporting included. |
| Exclusion Work | Every entry point sealed. Backed by a warranty — if anything gets back in through our sealing, we come back. |
| Disinfection and Cleanup | Attic and crawl space remediation after wildlife intrusion. Removes droppings, urine, nesting material, and odor. |
| Dead Animal Removal | Identification, removal, proper disposal, and odor elimination from walls, attics, crawl spaces, and yards. |
| Free Phone Consultation | Talk to a real technician before the visit. Describe what you are hearing or seeing. Get a clear picture of what to expect — no pressure, no obligation. |

### Peace-of-Mind Plans (Recurring Pest Protection)
- https://removeasap.com/peace-of-mind-from/beavers
- https://removeasap.com/peace-of-mind-from/bees-wasps-and-hornets
- https://removeasap.com/peace-of-mind-from/rodents

### Pricing Model
- Inspection + Credit: Initial inspection fee is credited toward service if you proceed.
- Service pricing is scope-based — determined after on-site inspection. Variables: animal type, extent of infestation, exclusion work needed, disinfection scope.
- Multiple repair options provided (good, better, best) so the homeowner can choose what fits the budget.
- Month-to-month, no long-term lock-in. Recurring Peace-of-Mind Plans available.
- Pricing is not published in advance — delivered after the inspection so the quote matches the actual problem.

### Risk Reversal — The ASAP Peace-of-Mind Promise

1. **Free phone consultation** — Speak with a real technician before any visit. No pressure, no obligation.
2. **Inspection credit** — The inspection fee is applied toward the full service if you proceed.
3. **Warranty on exclusion work** — If an animal gets back in through a sealed entry point, we come back.
4. **Multiple price options** — Choose the repair package that fits the budget. No one-size-fits-all quotes.
5. **Month-to-month, no long-term lock-in** — Service is earned on every visit.

## Why ASAP — Competitive Differentiation

- **Four-step complete solution** — inspect, trap, exclude, disinfect. Most competitors stop after step 2. That is why problems return.
- **24/6 emergency response** — Most pest companies are 9 to 5, Monday to Friday. Wildlife emergencies do not wait for business hours.
- **Humane and eco-friendly** — No poisons. No cruelty. Approved methods only. Safer for kids, pets, and the surrounding environment.
- **Licensed by Georgia DNR and Department of Agriculture** — Required credentials for legal wildlife handling in Georgia.
- **Warranty-backed exclusion** — Written warranty on sealing work. If they get back in, we come back.
- **Family-run** — Personal accountability. The owner answers the phone. Technicians are named and known.

## Target Customer Profile

### Residential
- Homeowners in Metro Atlanta and surrounding counties
- Single-family homes — often with attics, crawl spaces, or wooded lots
- Concerned about humane treatment of animals
- Worried about health risks from droppings, urine, and dead animals in the home
- Often calling after a previous removal company failed to seal entry points

### Commercial
- Property managers, apartment communities, retail spaces, offices
- Airports, hotels, schools, hospitals, food-service operations
- Require discreet service that does not alarm tenants or customers
- Need ongoing pest management plans with clear documentation

## Health Risk Education (Honest, Not Sensationalized)

- Raccoon droppings can carry roundworm (Baylisascaris procyonis).
- Bat guano in attics is associated with histoplasmosis.
- Rodent droppings and urine can transmit hantavirus and salmonella.
- Snake encounters in Georgia include venomous species (copperhead, rattlesnake, water moccasin).
- Cleanup and disinfection are not optional after wildlife intrusion — droppings and contaminated insulation must be removed by trained handlers.
